title: Practical 1
text:
Question 2: Summary Statistics
Calculate the mean, standard deviation, minimum, and maximum for the Temp (temperature) and Ozone variables in the airquality dataset.

#dataset contains null values so we must handle them too
mean(airquality$Temp, na.rm = TRUE)

[1] 77.88235

mean(airquality$Ozone, na.rm = TRUE)

[1] 42.12931

sd(airquality$Ozone, na.rm = TRUE)

[1] 32.98788

sd(airquality$Temp, na.rm = TRUE)

[1] 9.46527

min(airquality$Ozone, na.rm = TRUE)

[1] 1

min(airquality$Temp, na.rm = TRUE)

[1] 56

max(airquality$Ozone, na.rm = TRUE)

[1] 168

max(airquality$Temp, na.rm = TRUE)

[1] 97

title: Practical 1
text:
Question 3: Linear Regression via Matrices

Compute the regression coefficients using the matrix formula.


Y <- as.matrix(cars$dist)   
X<- cbind(1, cars$speed)

# solve(t(X) %*% X) 
beta_hat <- solve(t(X)%*%X)%*%t(X)%*%Y  ##linear regression
beta_hat 

           [,1]
[1,] -17.579095
[2,]   3.932409

title: Practical 1
section: Question 4: Verify Regression Coefficients with lm()
text:
Question 4: Verify Regression Coefficients with lm()
Verify that the matrix-based coefficients match those obtained using R’s built-in lm() function

lrm = lm(dist~speed, data= cars)
lrm$coefficients

(Intercept)       speed 
 -17.579095    3.932409

title: Introduction to Statistical Computing Exercises
text:
5 QQ-plot

set.seed(123)
x<- rt(1000, df=1) #cauchy distribution

#simulating theoretical quantiles using order stats
N<- length(x)
z<-sort(rnorm(100000, mean = 0, sd = 1))
p <- (1:N-0.5)/N
z_theoretical <- quantile(z,probs=p,type=1) 

x_sample <-sort(x)

B<- 1000
sim_mat <-matrix(NA,nrow = N, ncol = B)
for (b in 1:B) {
  sim_mat[,b]<-sort(rnorm(N)) #role of b
  
}

lower <-apply(sim_mat, 1, quantile, 0.025)
upper <- apply(sim_mat, 1, quantile, 0.975)
 
 
plot(z_theoretical,x_sample,
     pch=16, cex=0.6,
     ylim=c(-5,5),
     xlab= "Theoretical Normal Quantiles",
     ylab="Sample Quantiles",
     main = "QQ-plot vs Normal with 95% Envelope")
lines(z_theoretical,lower,col='firebrick',lty=2)
lines(z_theoretical,upper, col='firebrick', lty=2)
abline(0,1,col='blue')









library(car)

Loading required package: carData

qqPlot(x, envelope = 0.95, ylim = c(-5, 5))








[1] 727 868

title: Practical Day 4
text:
Question 2

Distances<- flights %>% filter(month==1)%>% 
            group_by(carrier) %>%                    # group_by also handles finding distinct values 
            summarise(mean_distance=mean(distance, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_distance = sd(distance, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
             arrange(mean_distance)
Distances

   carrier mean_distance sd_distance

title: Practical Day 4
text:
Question 3
We get sd = NA when there is only one observation and we get sd = 0 when all the observations are equal.

# Showing the chosen carriers sd and relevant information
carrier_data <- flights %>%
  filter(month == 1) %>%
  group_by(carrier) %>%
  summarise(
    num_flights = n(),
    unique_distances = n_distinct(distance),
    sd_dist = sd(distance)
  ) %>%
  # Filter for the cases specified:
  filter(num_flights == 1 | sd_dist == 0)

print(carrier_data)

# A tibble: 5 × 4
  carrier num_flights unique_distances sd_dist
  <chr>         <int>            <int>   <dbl>
1 AS               62                1       0
2 F9               59                1       0
3 HA               31                1       0
4 OO                1                1      NA
5 YV               46                1       0


Carrier OO has just one flight so its sd = NA. This is because n-1 is going to become 0 making the sd undefined. The rest of the carriers show that they have just one unique value showing that the distances are all the same value resulting in sd of 0.

title: Practical Day 4
text:
Question 6

dt_flights<- flights
dt_flights_route<-dt_flights %>% 
  group_by(origin,dest) %>%       #groups by routes
  filter(n_distinct(carrier)>1) %>%  #groups with more than 1 airline flies
  ungroup()
dt_flights_route

# A tibble: 276,735 × 19
    year month   day dep_time sched_dep_time dep_delay arr_time sched_arr_time
   <int> <int> <int>    <int>          <int>     <dbl>    <int>          <int>
 1  2013     1     1      542            540         2      923            850
 2  2013     1     1      554            600        -6      812            837
 3  2013     1     1      554            558        -4      740            728
 4  2013     1     1      555            600        -5      913            854
 5  2013     1     1      557            600        -3      709            723
 6  2013     1     1      557            600        -3      838            846
 7  2013     1     1      558            600        -2      753            745
 8  2013     1     1      558            600        -2      853            856
 9  2013     1     1      558            600        -2      924            917
10  2013     1     1      558            600        -2      923            937
# ℹ 276,725 more rows
# ℹ 11 more variables: arr_delay <dbl>, carrier <chr>, flight <int>,
#   tailnum <chr>, origin <chr>, dest <chr>, air_time <dbl>, distance <dbl>,
#   hour <dbl>, minute <dbl>, time_hour <dttm>



delay_flights <- dt_flights_route %>%
  group_by(origin,dest,carrier) %>% 
  summarise(average_arrival_delay= mean(arr_delay,na.rm = TRUE))%>% 
  left_join(airlines, by='carrier')

`summarise()` has grouped output by 'origin', 'dest'. You can override using
the `.groups` argument.

delay_flights

# A tibble: 343 × 5
# Groups:   origin, dest [128]
   origin dest  carrier average_arrival_delay name                    
   <chr>  <chr> <chr>                   <dbl> <chr>                   
 1 EWR    ATL   9E                      -6.25 Endeavor Air Inc.       
 2 EWR    ATL   DL                      10.00 Delta Air Lines Inc.    
 3 EWR    ATL   EV                      19.5  ExpressJet Airlines Inc.
 4 EWR    ATL   UA                      10.5  United Air Lines Inc.   
 5 EWR    AUS   UA                       4.28 United Air Lines Inc.   
 6 EWR    AUS   WN                     -11.2  Southwest Airlines Co.  
 7 EWR    BDL   EV                       6.78 ExpressJet Airlines Inc.
 8 EWR    BDL   UA                      22.6  United Air Lines Inc.   
 9 EWR    BNA   EV                      17.7  ExpressJet Airlines Inc.
10 EWR    BNA   WN                      -2.13 Southwest Airlines Co.  
# ℹ 333 more rows



#maximum avg. arrival delay for each route
worst_delay <- delay_flights %>%
  group_by(origin,dest) %>% 
  slice_max(average_arrival_delay,n=1,with_ties = FALSE )
worst_delay

# A tibble: 128 × 5
# Groups:   origin, dest [128]
   origin dest  carrier average_arrival_delay name                    
   <chr>  <chr> <chr>                   <dbl> <chr>                   
 1 EWR    ATL   EV                      19.5  ExpressJet Airlines Inc.
 2 EWR    AUS   UA                       4.28 United Air Lines Inc.   
 3 EWR    BDL   UA                      22.6  United Air Lines Inc.   
 4 EWR    BNA   EV                      17.7  ExpressJet Airlines Inc.
 5 EWR    BOS   B6                       6.87 JetBlue Airways         
 6 EWR    BWI   EV                      20.1  ExpressJet Airlines Inc.
 7 EWR    CHS   EV                      16.2  ExpressJet Airlines Inc.
 8 EWR    CLE   UA                       5.97 United Air Lines Inc.   
 9 EWR    CLT   EV                      20.5  ExpressJet Airlines Inc.
10 EWR    CVG   EV                      21.2  ExpressJet Airlines Inc.
# ℹ 118 more rows



# minimum avg. delay time for each route
best_delay <- delay_flights %>%
  group_by(origin, dest) %>%
  slice_min(average_arrival_delay, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)
best_delay

# A tibble: 128 × 5
# Groups:   origin, dest [128]
   origin dest  carrier average_arrival_delay name                    
   <chr>  <chr> <chr>                   <dbl> <chr>                   
 1 EWR    ATL   9E                     -6.25  Endeavor Air Inc.       
 2 EWR    AUS   WN                    -11.2   Southwest Airlines Co.  
 3 EWR    BDL   EV                      6.78  ExpressJet Airlines Inc.
 4 EWR    BNA   WN                     -2.13  Southwest Airlines Co.  
 5 EWR    BOS   EV                     -4.01  ExpressJet Airlines Inc.
 6 EWR    BWI   WN                      5.95  Southwest Airlines Co.  
 7 EWR    CHS   UA                    -14     United Air Lines Inc.   
 8 EWR    CLE   EV                     -3.71  ExpressJet Airlines Inc.
 9 EWR    CLT   US                      0.920 US Airways Inc.         
10 EWR    CVG   9E                      1.40  Endeavor Air Inc.       
# ℹ 118 more rows



# 
route_gaps <- bind_rows(worst_delay, best_delay) %>%
  group_by(origin, dest) %>%
  summarise(
    max_delay = max(average_arrival_delay),
    min_delay = min(average_arrival_delay),
    gap = max_delay - min_delay,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  slice_max(gap, n = 1)

route_gaps

# A tibble: 1 × 5
  origin dest  max_delay min_delay   gap
  <chr>  <chr>     <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>
1 JFK    ATL         128      1.40  127.



gap <- worst_delay %>% 
  bind_rows(best_delay) %>% 
  group_by(origin, dest) %>% 
  summarise(
    max_delay = max(average_arrival_delay),
    min_delay = min(average_arrival_delay),
    gap = max_delay - min_delay,
    worst_airline = carrier[which.max(average_arrival_delay)],
    best_airline  = carrier[which.min(average_arrival_delay)],
    .groups = "drop"
  )
greatest_gap <-gap %>% slice_max(gap)
greatest_gap

# A tibble: 1 × 7
  origin dest  max_delay min_delay   gap worst_airline best_airline
  <chr>  <chr>     <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl> <chr>         <chr>       
1 JFK    ATL         128      1.40  127. EV            9E          



flights %>% filter(origin=='JFK', dest=='ATL')

    year month   day dep_time sched_dep_time dep_delay arr_time sched_arr_time
   <int> <int> <int>    <int>          <int>     <dbl>    <int>          <int>
 1  2013     1     1      606            610        -4      837            845
 2  2013     1     1      807            810        -3     1043           1043
 3  2013     1     1     1325           1330        -5     1606           1605
 4  2013     1     1     1621           1548        33     1904           1830
 5  2013     1     1     1856           1855         1     2142           2142
 6  2013     1     2      606            610        -4      846            845
 7  2013     1     2      808            810        -2     1049           1045
 8  2013     1     2     1324           1330        -6     1552           1605
 9  2013     1     2     1551           1548         3     1838           1830
10  2013     1     2     1854           1855        -1     2126           2142
# ℹ 1,920 more rows
# ℹ 11 more variables: arr_delay <dbl>, carrier <chr>, flight <int>,
#   tailnum <chr>, origin <chr>, dest <chr>, air_time <dbl>, distance <dbl>,
#   hour <dbl>, minute <dbl>, time_hour <dttm>


Looking at the route data the huge difference could be caused by airline-specific operational factors such as scheduling, hub congestion, and fleet utilisation rather than route characteristics alone especially between 9E and DL.
